summary: EXEC_NPU_CMD converts scalar arguments through non-const lvalue references. Do not pass temporary casts like static_cast<float>(value); bind the scalar to a named local variable first, then pass that variable into the macro.

If a fused GEMM-style kernel returns almost all zeros while the reference is positive, first suspect weight layout and post-fusion logic. Passing Linear weights as transpose(0,1).contiguous() and moving avgpool/gelu/max out of the unreliable fused scalar kernel path restored correctness.

For Linear-derived custom ops on Ascend, keep the kernel focused on stable matmul+bias over explicit ND contiguous tensors, then do shape-sensitive post-processing separately when fused reduction code proves numerically wrong. This reduces debugging surface and preserves correctness.
